episodes.task_input_summary: Reminder email for Q2 All-Hands meeting mistakenly sent to support address, requesting RSVP.

task_understanding.notes: The task was understood as a need to clarify the misdirected email and respond accordingly.

workflow_step_feedback.notes: The agent recognized the importance of addressing a misdirected email promptly.
The agent accurately identified the context of the email as a mistake.
The response was appropriate as it clarified the situation without overstepping.
The response was clearly articulated and professional.
No escalation was necessary given the context, thus it cannot be assessed.

action_feedback.notes: The chosen action was appropriate as it addressed the misdirected email.

output_feedback.notes: The final output was well-structured, professional, and met the communication needs of the situation.

quality_feedback.notes: The communication style was professional and clear.
The tone was reassuring when addressing the mistake.

learning_signals: Continue to ensure clarity in communications regarding misdirected emails.
Reinforce the importance of context in responding to support tickets.

episodes.task_input_summary: Email intended for internal team regarding sprint 22 retrospective notes was sent to the support inbox by mistake.

task_understanding.notes: The agent correctly identified that the email was misdirected and addressed the situation appropriately.

workflow_step_feedback.notes: Urgency of the email was not explicitly evaluated in the stem agent output.
The agent recognized the context of internal communication and responded accordingly.
The response made it clear that the email was misdirected and provided guidance for next steps.
The response was clear and easy to understand, appropriately addressing the mistake.
No escalation was necessary in this case, and the episode did not present escalation as a requirement.

action_feedback.notes: The chosen action was suitable given the misdirected email; informing the sender to redirect their message was the correct approach.

output_feedback.notes: The response was professional, well-organized, and clear, aligning with the output policy.

quality_feedback.notes: The response was well-organized and tailored to the inquiry type.
The communication maintained a professional style.
The tone was reassuring, addressing the misdirection of the email effectively.

learning_signals: Continue maintaining clarity and professionalism in responses to misdirected communications.
Reinforce the importance of assessing urgency in future similar tasks.

episodes.task_input_summary: Meeting request follow-up from VendorCo's executive assistant seeking to schedule a follow-up meeting.

task_understanding.notes: The task of scheduling a follow-up meeting was understood accurately.

workflow_step_feedback.notes: The urgency of the meeting request was recognized.
The context of the original call and the need for scheduling was clearly understood.
The response included suitable options for meeting times and additional information as requested.
The communication was clear and professional.
No need for escalation observed in this case.

action_feedback.notes: The chosen action was suitable given the nature of the task, which was to respond to a support ticket.

output_feedback.notes: The output was well-organized, aligned with the goals of clear communication and professional tone.

quality_feedback.notes: The response was well-organized and relevant to the inquiry.
The communication was professional and complied with the adapted needs.
The tone was appropriate, maintaining professionalism and reassurance.

learning_signals: Maintain clarity and professionalism in responses to scheduling requests.
Ensure all relevant details are provided for meeting coordination.

recurrent_learning_signals: Continue to ensure clarity in communications regarding misdirected emails.
